content.title: Daily Schedule Conversation
content.body:
**Dialogue: Planning a Meeting**

A: 你明天上午有时间吗？(Nǐ míngtiān shàngwǔ yǒu shíjiān ma?)
*Do you have time tomorrow morning?*

B: 上午我九点有课。你几点有空？(Shàngwǔ wǒ jiǔ diǎn yǒu kè. Nǐ jǐ diǎn yǒu kōng?)
*I have class at 9:00 in the morning. What time are you free?*

A: 下午三点半怎么样？(Xiàwǔ sān diǎn bàn zěnmeyàng?)
*

content.title: Afternoon: 下午
content.body:
**下午** (xiàwǔ) means "afternoon" or "PM," referring to the time from after noon until evening (roughly 1:00 PM to 6:00 PM).

Place 下午 before the time: **下午[hour]点**.

Examples:
- 下午三点 (xiàwǔ sān diǎn) — 3:00 PM
- 下午五点半 (xiàwǔ wǔ diǎn bàn) — 5:30 PM

content.title: Basic Time Question Structure
content.body:
To ask "What time is it?" in Mandarin, use **几点了？** (jǐ diǎn le?), literally "how many o'clock?"

The question word **几** (jǐ) means "how many" or "what number," **点** (diǎn) means "o'clock" or "hour," and **了** (le) indicates a current state.

A more complete form adds **现在** (xiànzài, "now"): **现在

content.title: Morning Period: 上午
content.body:
**上午** (shàngwǔ) means "morning" or "AM," specifically referring to the time from early morning until noon.

Place 上午 before the time: **上午[hour]点**.

Examples:
- 上午八点 (shàngwǔ bā diǎn) — 8:00 AM
- 上午十点半 (shàngwǔ shí diǎn bàn) — 10:30 AM

上午 helps clarify whether you're referring to morning hours.

content.title: Adding Minutes with 分
content.body:
To express minutes, add **分** (fēn) after the number of minutes: **[hour]点[minutes]分**.

Examples:
- 三点十分 (sān diǎn shí fēn) — 3:10
- 五点二十分 (wǔ diǎn èrshí fēn) — 5:20
- 七点五分 (qī diǎn wǔ fēn) — 7:05

The character **分** (fēn) means "minute." Always state the hour first, then the minutes.

content.title: Expressing Half Past with 半
content.body:
To say "half past" an hour, use **半** (bàn) after the hour: **[hour]点半**.

Examples:
- 两点半 (liǎng diǎn bàn) — 2:30
- 九点半 (jiǔ diǎn bàn) — 9:30
- 十一点半 (shíyī diǎn bàn) — 11:30

**半** (bàn) means "half" and represents 30 minutes. Note: Use **两** (liǎng) for "2" when telling time, not 二 (èr).

content.title: Noon: 中午
content.body:
**中午** (zhōngwǔ) means "noon" or "midday," generally referring to the time around 12:00 PM and early afternoon (roughly 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM).

Examples:
- 中午十二点 (zhōngwǔ shí'èr diǎn) — 12:00 noon
- 中午 (zhōngwǔ) — at noon/lunchtime

中午 is commonly used when discussing lunch time.

content.title: Omitting 分 in Time Expressions
content.body:
In casual speech, **分** (fēn) can be omitted when stating minutes, though it's always correct to include it.

With 分: 三点十五分 (sān diǎn shíwǔ fēn)
Without 分: 三点十五 (sān diǎn shíwǔ)

Both mean "3:15" and are widely used. However:
- **半** (bàn) is never omitted: always say 三点半, never ~~三点三十~~
- In formal

content.title: Evening: 晚上
content.body:
**晚上** (wǎnshang) means "evening" or "night," referring to the time from early evening through nighttime (roughly 6:00 PM onwards).

Place 晚上 before the time: **晚上[hour]点**.

Examples:
- 晚上七点 (wǎnshang qī diǎn) — 7:00 PM
- 晚上十点半 (wǎnshang shí diǎn bàn) — 10:30 PM

晚上 is used for dinner time and nigh

content.title: Telling Hours with 点
content.body:
To tell the time in hours, use the pattern: **[number] + 点** (diǎn).

Examples:
- 一点 (yī diǎn) — 1 o'clock
- 三点 (sān diǎn) — 3 o'clock
- 五点 (wǔ diǎn) — 5 o'clock
- 十二点 (shí'èr diǎn) — 12 o'clock

Simply place the number (1-12) before 点 to indicate the hour. This forms the foundation of all time expr

content.title: Interpreting Time in Context
content.body:
**Notice: Library Hours**

图书馆开放时间：
上午九点到中午十二点
下午一点半到晚上八点

星期六、星期日：
上午十点到下午五点

*Library Opening Hours:
9:00 AM to 12:00 noon
1:30 PM to 8:00 PM

Saturday and Sunday:
10:00 AM to 5:00 PM*

content.title: Reading Analog and Digital Time
content.body:
When reading time displays:
- **Digital**: 14:30 → 下午两点半 or 下午两点三十分
- **Analog**: Clock showing 3:15 → 三点十五分 or 上午/下午三点十五分
- **12-hour format**: Use appropriate time period (上午/下午/晚上)
- **24-hour format**: Hours 13-24 use 下午 (13-18) or 晚上 (19-24); subtract 12 from the hour number: 19:00 → 晚上七点

Reme

content.title: Distinguishing Time Periods
content.body:
Choosing the correct time period word depends on the actual time of day:
- **上午** (shàngwǔ): morning, sunrise to noon (~6 AM - 12 PM)
- **中午** (zhōngwǔ): noon/lunchtime (~11 AM - 1 PM)
- **下午** (xiàwǔ): afternoon (~1 PM - 6 PM)
- **晚上** (wǎnshang): evening/night (~6 PM onwards)

There is some overla

content.title: Using 两 for "2 o'clock"
content.body:
When telling time, use **两** (liǎng) instead of 二 (èr) for the number 2 in hours.

Correct: **两点** (liǎng diǎn) — 2 o'clock
Incorrect: ~~二点~~

This is the same **两** used in 两个 (two of something). However, for minutes, you can use 二: 十二分 (12 minutes) is acceptable.

Examples:
- 两点 (liǎng diǎn) — 2:0

content.title: Asking About Daily Routines with 几点
content.body:
To ask "What time do you...?" use: **你几点[verb]？** (nǐ jǐ diǎn [verb]?)

Common examples:
- 你几点起床？(nǐ jǐ diǎn qǐchuáng?) — What time do you get up?
- 你几点睡觉？(nǐ jǐ diǎn shuìjiào?) — What time do you go to sleep?
- 你几点吃饭？(nǐ jǐ diǎn chīfàn?) — What time do you eat?

Answer with: **我[time][verb]** (wǒ [

content.title: Comparing Daily Schedules
content.body:
**Two Students' Schedules**

小明：
- 起床：上午六点半
- 上课：上午八点
- 吃午饭：中午十二点
- 睡觉：晚上十点

小红：
- 起床：上午七点十五分
- 上课：上午九点
- 吃午饭：中午十二点半  
- 睡觉：晚上十一点半

*Xiao Ming:
- Gets up: 6:30 AM
- Class starts: 8:00 AM
- Eats lunch: 12:00 noon
- Goes to sleep: 10:00 PM

Xiao Hong:
- Gets up: 7:15 AM
- Class starts: 9:00 AM
- Eats

content.title: Constructing Complete Time Expressions
content.body:
Complete time expressions combine time period + hour + minutes:
**[time period] + [hour]点 + [minutes]分/半**

Examples:
- 上午九点十五分 (shàngwǔ jiǔ diǎn shíwǔ fēn) — 9:15 AM
- 下午三点半 (xiàwǔ sān diǎn bàn) — 3:30 PM
- 晚上八点四十分 (wǎnshang bā diǎn sìshí fēn) — 8:40 PM

The time period always comes first, providin

content.title: Responding to Time Questions
content.body:
When asked 你几点...? respond with a complete sentence: **我[time period][hour]点([minutes]分/半)[verb]**.

Question: 你几点起床？(What time do you get up?)
Answer: 我上午七点起床。(I get up at 7:00 AM.)

Question: 你几点睡觉？(What time do you go to sleep?)
Answer: 我晚上十一点半睡觉。(I go to sleep at 11:30 PM.)

The time expression
